label: Linear models & generalized linear models

node.label: Linear models & generalized linear models
node.definition: Linear and generalized linear models predict through a weighted sum of features, optionally passed through a link function chosen for the target distribution.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Linear models & generalized linear models is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy to downstream capabilities including Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: substrate for Medical imaging & clinical decision support
substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.

dependencies.rationale: Linear models & generalized linear models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Linear models & generalized linear models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
Linear models & generalized linear models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust).
Linear models & generalized linear models follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
